summary: Soaring second set with Foolish Heart, Standing On The Moon & Mighty Quinn
blurb: The rain‑soaked first set is uneven and ghostly, but the second set erupts with a fiery Foolish Heart, an epic Standing On The Moon, and a triumphant Mighty Quinn finale. Jerry’s tone recovers after a shaky start while Vince’s keyboards sparkle on Samba In The Rain. Overall the band delivers a memorable night despite the downpour.
review:
On July 29, 1994 at Buckeye Lake Music Center the Dead fought through a literal storm to give fans a night that many still call the “Rain Show.” The opening number “Rain” set a drenched tone for a first set that reviewers describe as sluggish and even "ghostly" in Garcia’s playing. Yet the band kept the energy moving, slipping into classics like “Althea,” “Eternity,” and a tentative “Deal” before the clouds finally cleared.

When the second set kicked off with “Foolish Heart,” the atmosphere shifted dramatically. Jerry found his footing, delivering soaring solos that built into a monumental “Standing On The Moon.” Vince Welnick’s keyboards lit up the new “Samba In The Rain,” and the rhythm section of Bill and Mickey answered the thunder with punchy, energetic drumming. The night culminated in an exuberant encore – “The Mighty Quinn (Quinn The Eskimo)” – leaving the crowd drenched but elated. Despite the weather‑induced challenges, the performance’s high points make this show a standout entry in the ’94 catalog.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the second‑set highlights (Foolish Heart, Standing On The Moon, Mighty Quinn) while noting a weak, ghostly first set. Consensus is overall positive with strong consensus on standout moments, warranting a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Rich‑sounding audience tape with clear mids, good separation and no major dropouts; captures the soaring second set exceptionally well.

key_highlights: Second‑set surge featuring Foolish Heart, Standing On The Moon and Mighty Quinn.
Rain‑soaked atmosphere adds drama; first set is uneven but picks up toward the end.
Vince’s keyboards shine on new songs Samba In The Rain and Eternity.

band_performance.Jerry: Starts the night with a muted, ghost‑like tone but recovers dramatically in the second set, delivering soaring solos on Foolish Heart, Standing On The Moon and Mighty Quinn.
band_performance.Phil: Lays down a solid low end throughout, especially when Garcia falters; creates a comforting Phil Zone vibe for the band.
band_performance.Brent: Vince’s keyboards are clean and vibrant on Samba In The Rain and Eternity despite monitor complaints, providing strong harmonic support.
band_performance.Mickey: Pairs with Bill to deliver energetic, punchy drumming that answers the thunder of the rain‑filled night.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann adds driving force and dynamic fills, especially during the climactic second‑set jams.
